name: Ascent to the Dolmen of Sierra Gorda — circular from the Valle de la Serena
name_original: Subida al Dolmen de Sierra Gorda  — circular desde el Valle de la Serena
region_intro: Jogging around Valle De La Serena offers routes through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, valleys, and several "sierras" or mountain ranges. The terrain includes varied elevations, with paths ascending to notable points like Alto de la Cabra and the Dolmen of Sierra Gorda. Runners can expect a mix of challenging climbs and scenic stretches alongside natural features such as the Río and Sierra de la Gangosa. Best jogging routes around Valle De La Serena The most popular jogging route is Puerto Ancho – Puerto de la Cabra loop from Baños de la Sierra, a 16.7 miles (26.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route features the highest elevation gain, offering a demanding workout through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra de la Utrera and Castillo de, a difficult 14.2 miles (22.8 km) path. This route explores the Sierra de la Utrera, providing views of the local landscape and historical points. Local runners also love the Ascent to Alto de la Cabra — circular through Sierra de los Vuelos, a 12.3 miles (19.8 km) trail leading through mountain ranges and valleys, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Jogging around Valle De La Serena is defined by its sierras, valleys, and river systems, offering diverse landscapes for runners. The network includes options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore Valle De La Serena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Valle De La Serena?
A: There are 9 running routes available in Valle De La Serena, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find options ranging from moderate to difficult, with 1 easy, 3 moderate, and 5 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running trails in Valle De La Serena?
A: Yes, there is one easy running route available. While many trails feature challenging climbs, you can find options suitable for beginners, particularly if you're looking for less strenuous paths through the valleys.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of running routes in Valle De La Serena?
A: The running routes in Valle De La Serena vary significantly in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283332279/ascent-to-the-dolmen-of-sierra-gorda-circular-from-the-valle-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Dolmen of Sierra Gorda&lt;/a&gt; is about 13.1 km (8.1 miles) and takes around 1 hour 40 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Ancho – Puerto de la Cabra loop&lt;/a&gt; is 26.8 km (16.7 miles) and can take over 3.5 hours.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Valle De La Serena?
A: Yes, many of the running paths in Valle De La Serena are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283295243/ascent-to-alto-de-la-cabra-circular-through-sierra-de-los-vuelos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Alto de la Cabra — circular through Sierra de los Vuelos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284009405/circular-sierra-de-la-gangosa-and-rio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Sierra de la Gangosa and Río&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Valle De La Serena?
A: The terrain in Valle De La Serena is characterized by rolling hills, valleys, and several mountain ranges (sierras). Runners can expect a mix of challenging climbs and scenic stretches, with paths ascending to notable points like Alto de la Cabra and the Dolmen of Sierra Gorda. You'll encounter varied elevations and natural features such as the Río and Sierra de la Gangosa.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running routes in Valle De La Serena?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural areas like Valle De La Serena, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always recommended to check local regulations or specific route details for any restrictions, particularly in protected areas or near livestock.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while running in Valle De La Serena?
A: Valle De La Serena offers several interesting landmarks along its routes. You might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3162049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Sierra Gorda&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Pajosa&lt;/a&gt;. Natural viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2458178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabra Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; also offer stunning views.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of the Sierra de la Utrera?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283320997/sierra-de-la-utrera-and-castillo-de" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Utrera and Castillo de&lt;/a&gt; route specifically explores the Sierra de la Utrera, providing expansive views of the local landscape and historical points within the region.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Valle De La Serena?
A: Runners often praise the diverse landscapes and challenging terrain of Valle De La Serena. The komoot community highlights the rewarding climbs and the opportunity to explore various sierras, valleys, and river systems, making for a varied and engaging running experience.

Q: Is Valle De La Serena suitable for family-friendly running or walking?
A: While many routes are rated moderate to difficult due to elevation changes, the region does offer some less strenuous paths. Families looking for gentler options might consider shorter sections of trails or the single easy route available, focusing on the flatter valley areas rather than the demanding mountain ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Valle De La Serena?
A: The best time to go running in Valle De La Serena is generally during the spring and autumn months. These seasons offer milder temperatures, making the varied terrain more enjoyable for longer runs. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions, especially at higher elevations.
tips: Nucleus that is close to the Sierras del Arrozao on the one hand and the Río Guadámez on the other. The closest, the Sierra de la Dehesa to the north of the town.

One of the most representative heritage elements of Valle de la Serena is this dolmen, a funerary-type construction of the dolmen culture, in the Eneolithic period. (1,500 BC) It is made with large stones that abound in the place, driven vertically and attached to each other forming a circular mound of about 4 m in diameter which is accessed by a corridor.
